latex:
If 6 chickens lay 48 eggs in 4 days, how many days will it take 10 chickens to lay 100 eggs?

}

\textbf{Answer:} This is an inverse proportion problem. The general form of an inverse proportion equation is $xy =k$, where $k$ is a constant.  In this case, allowing $x$ to represent the number of chickens and $y$ the number of days, we have $6\cdot4 = 48\cdot k$.  $k=0.5$, meaning it takes 0.5 chicken-days to lay one egg. To find the number of days it would take 10 chickens to lay 100 eggs, we could solve $10y=0.5\cdot 100$ for $y$.\\

Many students may find it easier to make a table, like this:\\

\begin{tabular}{c|c|c}
Chickens & Days & Eggs\\
\hline
6&4 & 48\\
6&1 &12\\
1&1&2\\
1&50&100\\
10&5&100\\
\end{tabular}\\
\\
Using either method, we have $\boxed{5}$ days.
